title: [Pidgeot] Battle Guide
summary: A [Normal]/[Flying] utility [pivot] with Speed 101, [roost], and [defog] — the skeleton of a physical [pivot] core.
strengths: Unboosted Speed 101 [pivot]s cleanly through mid-tier attackers
[defog] clears hazards without burning an offense slot
[roost] + [u-turn] keeps HP stable through long [pivot] loops
weaknesses: 80 Attack gives weak finishing power against walls
Ability choices are all mediocre competitively

recommendedMoves.reason: Pivot bug move that preserves tempo

recommendedItems.reason: One-shot healing for critical early turns

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Pidgeot] is a mid-Speed [Normal]/[Flying] utility [pivot] that brings Speed 101, [roost], and [defog] to a single slot. Its stats are plain and its damage output is low, but the ability to handle hazard control and [pivot] tempo from one team slot gives it a legitimate home on balance cores in the Champions format.

## Strengths
Unboosted 101 Speed sits above a lot of the mid-tier metagame, which keeps [u-turn] pivoting reliable. [defog] is the utility that pays rent — any offense team that wants to avoid [stealth-rock] chip appreciates a dedicated remover. Combined with [roost], [Pidgeot] can manage its HP turn after turn, and [brave-bird] recoil is directly undone by the healing slot.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[Normal]/[Flying] carries [Rock], [Electric], and [Ice] weaknesses all at once, and the 4x [stealth-rock] damage is punishing without a dedicated remover already on the field. 80 Attack struggles to break walls, and none of the three abilities are truly competitive — big-pecks is the best choice because it prevents [intimidate] and Defense drops. Lean on [pivot] positioning instead of trying to break through bulk.

## Recommended Moveset
[brave-bird] is the main [Flying] STAB because a committed damage slot still matters even on a [pivot] set. [u-turn] is the core [pivot] move that keeps team tempo alive. [roost] is mandatory recovery to offset [brave-bird] recoil and enable the long-game plan. The last slot is [defog] for hazard control — it reduces the whole team dependency on a separate remover. [heat-wave] and [hurricane] variants exist but the utility set is stronger here.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[leftovers] is the default item because the [roost] + [leftovers] combo maximises long-game healing. [sharp-beak] is the offensive variant that turns [brave-bird] into a real attack slot, and [sitrus-berry] is the one-shot heal choice. Ability stays big-pecks to prevent Defense drops. Tera [Flying] preserves STAB damage, while Tera [Steel] flips the [Rock] and [Ice] weaknesses into resistances.

## Teammates & Synergy
[Pidgeot] is a glue [pivot] and [defog] user, so it pairs with [Ground] or [Steel] tanks that [soak] physical attacks from the other side of the board. A [Ground] partner covers the [Electric] weakness, and a [wish] user behind [Pidgeot] extends the long-game healing plan. Setup sweepers behind the [u-turn] loop gain safe entries on free turns.

## Play Patterns
Lead [Pidgeot] into a predicted passive matchup to start the [defog] + [u-turn] loop, then [roost] to [recover] before cycling again. Use [brave-bird] only on guaranteed KOs to minimise recoil accumulation, and Tera on any telegraphed [Rock] or [Electric] attack.
